title: Dynamic Policy Fusion for User Alignment Without Re-Interaction
problem: Users may desire policies that deviate from optimal policies to accommodate personal preferences, but traditional approaches require retraining RL agents from scratch, which is challenging and inefficient.
approach: A policy fusion-based approach to learn a personalized policy that respects human preferences while satisfying task objectives.

methodology.trajectory_scoring: Trajectories used to learn the task are numerically scored to reflect alignment with human preferences
methodology.intent_inference: LSTM is used to infer user intent and construct an intent-specific policy
methodology.policy_fusion: The task-specific policy is fused with the intent-specific policy
methodology.bounded_divergence: The approach ensures bounded divergence from the task-specific policy
methodology.zero_shot: No additional environment interactions are required

contributions: Policy fusion-based approach for personalized policy learning without retraining
Identification of limitations with static policy fusion (dominance issues)
Dynamic policy fusion approach that regulates dominance by adjusting temperature parameter of Boltzmann distribution
applications: Navigation scenarios where users prefer avoiding toll roads or scenic routes
theoretical_guarantees: Bounded divergence of personalized policy with respect to task-specific policy
